findings.description: LND gRPC backend (src/lightning/lnd.ts) has all 5 methods throwing 'LND backend not implemented — Phase 1'. The package.json describes this as a 'Production TypeScript Cashu mint — Chaumian ecash backed by Lightning', but no real Lightning backend exists. Only FakeWallet (in-memory test shim) is functional.
findings.recommendation: Implement LndBackend using @grpc/grpc-js: connect to LND with TLS cert + macaroon, implement createInvoice (AddInvoice RPC), subscribeInvoices (SubscribeInvoices streaming RPC), decodePayReq (DecodePayReq RPC), estimateFee (EstimateRouteFee or QueryRoutes RPC), and sendPayment (SendPaymentSync RPC).

findings.description: NUT-05 melt flow has an acknowledged irrecoverable-loss bug: if sendPayment() fails after proofs are atomically spent (spendProofsAndSignAtomically succeeds), proofs remain permanently burned with no compensation. Comment in mint-service.ts:459 notes 'ideally should unspend proofs, but for Phase 1 we mark the quote as UNPAID (proofs are already spent though)'. This breaks the atomicity guarantee for melt.
findings.recommendation: Implement a two-phase commit for melt: (1) mark proofs as PENDING before payment, (2) burn them only after payment success, or implement proof reversal on payment failure. Consider using DB-level PENDING state for input proofs during in-flight melt operations.

findings.description: NUT-07 checkProofState (src/services/mint-service.ts:474) only returns SPENT or UNSPENT states. The NUT-07 spec requires a third PENDING state for proofs locked in an active melt quote. Proofs submitted to a melt that is in-flight (PENDING Lightning payment) should return state='PENDING', not UNSPENT.
findings.recommendation: Track proof PENDING state by storing input proofs when a melt is initiated (before sendPayment). Query both SpentProof table and a PendingProof table (or use a state field) in checkProofState to return all three states correctly.

findings.description: NUT-02 claims keyset rotation support, but only one keyset is ever created at init() and no rotation API or mechanism exists. The ROADMAP mentions 'Keyset rotation: old keysets stay spendable (redeem) but not issuable (new mints)' but there is no POST /v1/keys/rotate endpoint, no rotation trigger, and no multi-keyset loading from DB.
findings.recommendation: Add a keyset rotation mechanism: derive a new keyset (index+1), mark old keyset inactive in DB, load both into in-memory map so old proofs remain verifiable. Optionally expose an admin endpoint or config-driven rotation.

findings.description: Integration tests against a cashu-ts wallet client are listed as a remaining Phase 1 item in ROADMAP.md but are completely absent. There are no tests simulating real wallet-to-mint protocol flows (blind/unblind round-trips using cashu-ts primitives, NUT-compliant serialization, token V3/V4 format compatibility).
findings.recommendation: Add a test suite in src/integration/__tests__/ using cashu-ts wallet library (or wallet-sdk) to verify end-to-end Cashu flows: mint → swap → melt with real cashu-ts blind/unblind operations and proof serialization.
findings.feature_name: Integration tests against cashu-ts wallet client

findings.description: NUT-08 (Lightning fee return / blank output signing for change) is partially implemented in meltTokens service logic — it accepts optional change outputs and signs them if provided — but is not declared in SUPPORTED_NUTS (src/core/constants.ts) and thus not advertised to wallets in GET /v1/info. Wallets cannot discover this capability.
findings.recommendation: Either complete NUT-08 by advertising it in SUPPORTED_NUTS (add key '8' with methods), or remove the partial change-output handling to avoid confusion. If advertising, also add Zod validation and route-level tests for the change output flow.
findings.feature_name: NUT-08: Lightning fee return (undisclosed partial implementation)

findings.description: generateDLEQ() in src/core/crypto/bdhke.ts throws 'Not implemented — Phase 2'. It is exported and tested (the test asserts the throw), but it is wired into the DLEQProof type on BlindSignature. If any code path ever sets dleq on a BlindSignature, it will silently send undefined to the wallet. Phase 2 DLEQ is correctly marked as future work in ROADMAP.
findings.recommendation: Implement Schnorr DLEQ proof in Phase 2 as planned: generate random r, compute R1=r*G, R2=r*B_, e=hash(R1,R2,K,C_), s=r+e*k. Guard the function call sites so they only generate DLEQ when the feature flag is enabled.

findings.description: getMintQuote / mintTokens use a fragile FakeWallet-specific payment detection hack (extractPaymentHash at line 641-662 in mint-service.ts). The logic relies on string-slicing a fake bolt11 and padding with zeros to reconstruct a payment hash, which is tightly coupled to FakeWallet's internal format. A real LND backend would require a different polling or subscription mechanism, and the current getMintQuote has no LND invoice subscription wiring.
findings.recommendation: Replace the FakeWallet polling hack with a proper invoice subscription in the mint startup flow: call subscribeInvoices() and update quote state via DB when a settled invoice is received, regardless of backend type. This also removes the FakeWallet type cast in getMintQuote.

context.summary: The cashu-mint Phase 1 implementation is largely complete for the protocol-layer NUTs (00, 01, 03, 06) with solid crypto primitives and tested business logic using FakeWallet. However, two significant gaps undermine its production readiness: (1) the LND gRPC backend is entirely stubbed — all five methods throw — meaning the mint cannot process real Lightning payments, contradicting the package.json claim of 'Production TypeScript Cashu mint backed by Lightning'; (2) NUT-05 melt has a known atomicity bug where Lightning payment failures result in irrecoverable proof loss. NUT-07 is incomplete (no PENDING state), NUT-02 keyset rotation is unimplemented despite being claimed, and the absence of cashu-ts wallet interoperability tests means real-world wallet compatibility is unverified. The codebase architecture is clean and well-structured — DDD layers are respected, saga recovery for swap/mint is solid, and 33 unit tests pass — but Phase 1 should not be considered production-ready until LND is implemented and the melt atomicity issue is resolved.
